channel.title: Home on CSC-324 Competencies Page
channel.description: Recent content in Home on CSC-324 Competencies Page

item.title: Development
item.description:
Development Competencies

Medium-Scale Abstraction

Medium-Scale Architecture

item.title: Collaboration
item.description:
Collaboration

Instance 1

  • Work done:

  • Issue:

Instance 2

  • Work done:

  • Issue:

Instance 3

  • Work done:

  • Issue:

Collaboration Difficulties?

item.title: Evaluation
item.description:
Evaluation

What was the design of your user study to evaluate your artifact?

What were the results of this user study?

What were the main points of future development that you took away from this activity?

item.title: Infrastructure
item.description:
Infrastructure

What development tools did you use through process to help write code and automate the build/deployment process?

What is one specific problem (e.g., debugging and issue) that you encountered while writing code and how did your tools help or hinder your ability to address that problem?

item.title: Medium-Scale Abstraction
item.description:
Medium-Scale Abstraction

What functionality does your code sample abstract away?

How does it mechanically achieve abstraction?

What purpose does this abstraction serve in your larger program?

item.title: Medium-Scale Architecture
item.description:
Medium-Scale Architecture

What architectural pattern does your program employ?

What components result from this pattern in your program?

What technologies and/or libraries make-up each of the components?

item.title: Needfinding
item.description:
Needfinding

What techniques did you employ to discover users’ needs?

As a group, we conducted multiple interviews and observations of our peers to learn how they tend to interact with idle games. Personally, I conducted one open-ended interview and one idle game observation. In the interview, I let the person talk freely about their view on idle games and prompted with more questions as needed; in the observation, I had the participant play an idle game they had never played before and narrate their thought process and actions as they played.

item.title: Process
item.description:
Process

What software engineering practices did your group employ to manage, distribute, and execute on their work?

In your estimation, how well did your group follow through on using these processes throughout the semester?

What, if anything, got in the way of integrating these process into your workflow?

item.title: Prototyping
item.description:
Prototyping

What design questions did your intend to answer with your prototype(s)?

Could you tell what you needed to do?

Do the starting mechanisms feel appropriate?

Is the initial story compelling and logical?

Does the medium of the story match with the mechanisms?

How did you design your prototype(s) and subsequent user studies to address these questions?

We made our prototype a mock tutorial of our game, with the main production centered around papyrus –> paper instead of wood –> paper. This allowed us to mock up the start of the game, as the introduction of idle mechanics was something we wanted to focus on. We had little paper signs and scripted instructions to guide players. We also gave players a small story summary before playing so they would have an idea of the greater context.

item.title: Self-Learning
item.description:
Self-Learning

Describe the technology that the tutorial addresses and how it fits into your project.

Evaluate how useful the tool was in your work. What were its strengths and weaknesses, especially in comparison with other tools you have used.

Identify one particular sticking point to using this technology that you would want your team to know about when adopting the tool and how you resolved it.

item.title: Testing Infrastructure
item.description:
Testing Infrastructure

How have you automated your tests so that they both take “1-click” to run and run automatically as part of build validation?

Describe a specific occurrence in which your testing infrastructure saved you and/or your team work.

item.title: Testing Strategies
item.description:
Testing Strategies

According to code coverage tools, what aspects of this component are covered with tests?

What kinds of tests are they?

For code that is not covered by tests, why did you not cover them?
